name: Achieve Your Ambitious Mom Life | Women in STEM, Work-Life Balance, Career Goals, Career Planning, Decision Making
host: Anokhi Kapasi, PhD - Life Planning & Career Strategist for Women in STEM
description:
Have you ever been told that work-life balance for women in STEM is a myth?
Do you want a meaningful science career and a rooted family life —but you worry that you’re “failing at both”?
Are you running low on time, energy, and sleep, trying to keep up with grant deadlines, conferences, and kids?
Do you wonder if negotiating career shifts, flexible jobs, STEM-adjacent roles, or maternity leave is proof you’re not a serious scientist?
Welcome to Science Careers for Moms — a TOP 2.5% GLOBALLY RANK

recent_episodes.title: EP 110: Clear Decision Making Comes From Rewiring Your Identity as an Ambitious Woman [Neurosomatic Practitioner - Riana Malia]
recent_episodes.description:
Identity Shift for Women starts when you stop making decisions from an old version of yourself.

If you’re a mom in STEM who’s successful on paper but disconnected inside, this episode will help you make clear decision-making choices without self-blame.

Riana Malia is a board-certified neurosomatic practitioner and identity architect with 20+ years in personal development. She helps high-achieving women regulate their nervous system, clear old patterns, and make aligned decisions withou

recent_episodes.title: EP 109: The Hidden Time Management Problem Sabotaging Work‑Life Balance for Moms in STEM
recent_episodes.description:
Time management for working moms in STEM shouldn't feel this hard. You get everything done—code compiles, data collected, manuscript revised—but at home? There's no clear finish line. Just that nagging feeling you could always do one more thing.

In today's episode, I'm breaking down a simple 4-part framework that will help you define "done" intentionally so you can stop overworking and start experiencing real balance. This isn't about lowering your standards—it's about reallocating exc

recent_episodes.title: EP 108: Work-Life Balance Question: What Does “Being Aligned” Really Mean as a Scientist Mom?
recent_episodes.description:
Work-life balance for working moms in STEM feels like constant juggling—lab deadlines, family responsibilities, career demands, and that nagging feeling you're failing at all of it.

And somehow you're still supposed to pursue meaningful work and be present with your family?

This episode is your clarity.

I'm walking you through what "being aligned" really means for scientist moms—not the vague Instagram version, but the concrete kind where your values, vision, and daily de

contact_name: Anokhi Kapasi, PhD - Life Planning & Career Strategist for Women in STEM
score_reason: Targets women in STEM with work-life balance focus, not the business decision-maker or marketing professional audience Andreas serves.
